content.full_text:
Law Offices of

OLSON, CONNELLY, HAGEL, FONG & LEIDIGH


March 20, 1990


Kathryn E. Donovan, Esq.
General Counsel
Fair Political Practices Commission
428 J Street, Suite 800
Sacramento, CA 95814

Re: Advice letter request

Dear Ms. Donovan:

     This office represents Attorney General John Van
de Kamp. In addition, we represent three committees
for which Attorney General Van de Kamp is the
proponent: Californians for Cleaning Out Drug Dealers,
Californians for Cleaning Up Government, and
Californians for Cleaning Up the Environment
("Committees"). The purpose of each Committee is to
support the qualification and passage of an initiative
measure. On behalf of our clients, we are seeking
advice pursuant to Government Code Section 83114 (b)
concerning the campaign reporting provisions of the
Political Reform Act.

     We request your confirmation of the Commission's
determination at its March 13, 1990 meeting that the
contribution limitations in Proposition 73 do not apply
to contributions received by candidate-controlled
ballot measure committees. (Government Code sections
85301-85302, 85305.) We also request your confirmation
of the related Commission determination to reverse the
advice given in the Hiltachk Advice Letter (A-89-533)
and to henceforth advise that transfers of campaign
funds from a candidate-controlled committee to a ballot
measure committee controlled by another candidate are
not prohibited by the Political Reform Act.
(Government Code section 85304.)

     Thank you for your attention to this matter.

                                        Sincerely,

                                        OLSON, CONNELLY, HAGEL, FONG & LEIDIGH


                                        [signature]
                                        DIANE M. FISHBURN

                                        DMF:dr

300 Capitol Mall, Suite 350
Sacramento, California 95814
TELEPHONE (916) 442-2952
FAX: (916) 442-1280

LANCE H. OLSON
BRUCE J. HAGEL
LEROY Y. FONG
EUGENE T. LEIDIGH
GEORGE M. WATERS

OF COUNSEL
LLOYD G. CONNELLY, Member
California State Legislature

Law Offices of

OLSON, CONNELLY, HAGEL, FONG & LEIDIGH

MAR 22 2:18 PM '90

March 20, 1990


Kathryn E. Donovan, Esq.
General Counsel
Fair Political Practices Commission
428 J Street, Suite 800
Sacramento, CA 95814

RE: Advice letter request

Dear Ms. Donovan:

     This office represents the California
Environmental Protection Act of 1990, Hayden Committee
("Committee") and Assemblymember Tom Hayden. On behalf
of our clients, we are seeking advice pursuant to
Government Code Section 83114(b) concerning the
campaign reporting provisions of the Political Reform
Act.

     We request your confirmation of the determination
of the Commission at its March 13, 1990 meeting that
the contribution limitations in Proposition 73 do not
apply to contributions received by candidate-controlled
ballot measure committees. (Government Code Sections
85301-85303, 85305.) We also request confirmation of
the related Commission determination to reverse the
advice given in the Hiltachck Advice Letter (A-89-533)
and to henceforth advise that transfers of campaign
funds from a candidate-controlled committee to a ballot
measure committee controlled by another candidate are
not prohibited by the Political Reform Act.
(Government Code section 85304.)

LANCE H. OLSON
BRUCE J. HAGEL
LEROY Y. FONG
ROBERT E. LEIDIGH
GEORGE M. WATERS

OF COUNSEL
LLOYD G. CONNELLY, Member
California State Legislature

     Thank you for your attention to this matter.

Sincerely,

OLSON, CONNELLY, HAGEL, FONG & LEIDIGH

DIANE M. FISHBURN

DMF:dr

300 Capitol Mall, Suite 350
Sacramento, California 95814
TELEPHONE (916) 442-2952
FAX: (916) 442-1280

Law Offices of

OLSON, CONNELLY, HAGEL, FONG & LEIDIGH

MAR 22 2:28 IN '90

March 20, 1990


Kathryn E. Donovan, Esq.
General Counsel
Fair Political Practices Commission
428 J Street, Suite 800
Sacramento, CA 95814

Re: Advice letter request

Dear Ms. Donovan:

     This office represents Lt. Governor Leo McCarthy,
a candidate for re-election on the 1990 ballot. This
office also represents Californians for Safe Streets, a
recipient committee, which is organized to support the
qualification and passage of an initiative measure.
On behalf of our clients, we are seeking advice
pursuant to Government Code Section 83114(b) concerning
the campaign reporting provisions of the Political
Reform Act.

     We request your confirmation of the Commission's
determination that the contribution limitations in
Proposition 73 do not apply to contributions received
by candidate-controlled ballot measure committees.
(Government Code Sections 85301-85303, 85305.) We also
request your confirmation of the related Commission
determination to reverse the advice given in the
Hiltachk Advice Letter (A-89-533) and to henceforth
advise that transfers of campaign funds from a
candidate-controlled committee to a ballot measure
committee controlled by another candidate are not
prohibited by the Political Reform Act. (Government
Code section 85304.)

     Thank you for your attention to this matter.

Sincerely,

OLSON, CONNELLY, HAGEL, FONG & LEIDIGH

[signature]
DIANE M. FISHBURN

DMF:dr

LANCE H. OLSON
BRUCE J. HAGEL
LEROY Y. FONG
ROBERT E. LEIDIGH
GEORGE M. WATERS

OF COUNSEL
LLOYD G. CONNELLY, Member
California State Legislature

300 Capitol Mall, Suite 350
Sacramento, California 95814
TELEPHONE: (916) 442-2952
FAX: (916) 442-1280
